title: S.A.M Monster
description: A custom monster for a Support-All-Mods content pack. IMPORTANT: Barony has a fixed set of 48 creature types (sprite/AI/limbs are hard-coded) and cannot add brand-new ones. A S.A.M monster is a VARIANT of an existing type ('base_type') with your own stats, equipment, inventory, name, proficiencies, and behaviour flags. S.A.M writes this file into Barony's data/custom-monsters/ and, from the 'spawn' block, generates the data/monstercurve.json spawn table so the monster actually appears in play.

$schema.description: Optional. URL of this JSON Schema, so editors (VS Code, etc.) give autocomplete + validation as you type. Ignored by S.A.M itself.

id.description: Unique id in 'namespace:monster' form (e.g. 'darkblade:goblin_captain'). The namespace must match your mod's namespace. S.A.M writes the on-disk file as '<namespace>_<monster>.json' so ids never collide across mods.

name.description: Display name of this monster variant (e.g. 'Goblin Captain'). Shown in-game unless 'properties.display_as_generic_species' is true.

base_type.description: Which existing Barony creature this variant is built from. Its sprite, model, limbs, and AI come from this base type; you override the stats/gear/name/behaviour. A typo here would silently produce a 'nothing' monster in vanilla Barony — S.A.M rejects an unknown base_type at load with the list of valid names.

sex.description: 0 = male, 1 = female. Affects some sprites/voices for the base type.

appearance.description: Cosmetic appearance index passed to the base type (varies per monster; leave unset for the default).

stats.description: Base attributes. Any omitted stat keeps the base type's default.

EXP.description: Starting experience toward its next level.

random_stats.description: Random variance added on spawn (a value of N adds a random 0..N to the matching stat). Leave 0 for a fixed statline.

proficiencies.description: Skill levels 0-100. Keys must be Barony's skill display names: Tinkering, Stealth, Trading, Lore, Thaumaturgy, Leader, Mysticism, Sorcery, Ranged, Sword, Mace, Axe, Polearm, Shield, Unarmed, Alchemy. (Thaumaturgy/Mysticism/Sorcery are the three spellcasting schools.) Unknown keys are warned + dropped at load.

equipped_items.description: Gear the monster spawns wearing/wielding. Each slot is a single item, or an array of items for a weighted random pick. Unlisted slots use the base type's default gear only if 'properties.fill_empty_equipment_with_defaults' is true.

inventory_items.description: Carried (not equipped) items, e.g. spellbooks it can cast (see 'enable_casting_inventory_spellbooks'), potions, or loot to drop. Each entry is one item, or an array for a weighted random pick.

display_as_generic_species.description: If true, show the generic species name (e.g. 'skeleton') instead of 'name'.

fill_empty_equipment_with_defaults.description: If true, slots you did NOT specify are filled with the base type's default gear. If false, unspecified slots are empty.

populate_default_inventory.description: If true, the base type's default inventory is added on top of your 'inventory_items'.

disable_miniboss_chance.description: If true, this monster can never roll the random miniboss upgrade.

force_player_recruitable.description: If true, players can always recruit this monster (bypasses the normal recruit check).

force_player_friendly.description: If true, spawns allied to the players.

force_player_enemy.description: If true, spawns hostile to the players even if the base type would be neutral.

disable_item_drops.description: If true, drops no items on death (overrides drop chances).

xp_award_percent.description: Percent of normal XP awarded for killing it.

enable_casting_inventory_spellbooks.description: If true, the monster casts spells from spellbooks in its 'inventory_items'.

followers.description: Minions spawned alongside this monster as its followers.

follower_variants.description: Weighted map of follower monsters to pick from. Keys are OTHER S.A.M monsters in 'namespace:monster' form (S.A.M rewrites them to the on-disk filename); use 'default' for a plain vanilla monster. e.g. { "darkblade:rat_swarm": 3, "default": 1 }.

shopkeeper_properties.description: Only meaningful when base_type is 'shopkeeper'. Controls the shop this keeper runs.

store_type_chances.description: Weighted map of store types the keeper may run. Keys: equipment, hats, jewelry, books, apothecary, staffs, food, hardware, hunting, general.

generate_default_shop_items.description: If true, stock the shop with the usual generated items for its store type.

spawn.description: Where this monster appears in the world. S.A.M merges these into data/monstercurve.json. NOTE: listing a level makes S.A.M manage that level's random spawn table — the level then spawns the species that loaded mods list (plus a 'default' weight that keeps the vanilla version of each listed species appearing). Levels you don't list keep vanilla spawning untouched. Leave 'spawn' empty to only appear via followers, map-editor placement (entity name ending .json), or the console.

level_name.description: Barony map name this monster spawns on (e.g. 'The Mines', 'The Swamp', 'The Labyrinth'). Free-form; S.A.M soft-warns on names it doesn't recognise so mod-added levels still work.

mode.description: 'random' = part of the level's random monster generation (uses weighted_chance + depth). 'fixed' = attached to the level's fixed/scripted spawns.

base_species.description: Which vanilla species slot in the level's spawn table this variant attaches to. Defaults to 'base_type'. Keep equal to base_type unless you know what you're doing.

weighted_chance.description: For 'random' mode: how often this species is chosen among the level's spawnable species.

dungeon_depth_minimum.description: Earliest floor (within this level's biome) the monster can appear on.

dungeon_depth_maximum.description: Latest floor the monster can appear on.

variant_weight.description: When this species rolls, how often the pick is THIS custom variant.

default_weight.description: When this species rolls, how often the pick is the plain vanilla monster (keep > 0 so vanilla versions still spawn alongside your variant).

itemEntry.description: One item. Most fields accept either a single value or an array (a random pick is made from the array on spawn).

type.description: Vanilla item name (lowercase, e.g. 'magicstaff_lightning', 'hat_wizard', 'spellbook_cold'), an array of names for a random pick, or 'empty' for no item. Unknown names are warned at load and fall back to a default item.

status.description: Condition: broken, decrepit, worn, serviceable, or excellent (single or array).

appearance.description: Appearance index, an array of indices, or the literal 'random'.

spawn_percent_chance.description: Chance (0-100) this item spawns at all.

drop_percent_chance.description: Chance (0-100) the item drops on death.

slot_weighted_chance.description: Relative weight when this entry is one option in an array (weighted random pick).

itemSlot.description: A single item, or an array of items for a weighted random pick (weights via 'slot_weighted_chance').
